privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: How we handle information in {platform}. This is a concise product policy—have your counsel adapt it for your jurisdiction and deployment.
privacy.p1: {platform} is a multi-tenant business operations platform. Your organization’s data is logically separated by workspace (company). This policy describes categories of information the product is designed to process and how operators typically configure it.
privacy.li1: Account identifiers such as name, email, and role for each teammate.
privacy.li2: Business records you enter: customers, suppliers, products, documents, invoices, payments, and uploaded files (e.g. logos, receipts, images).
privacy.li3: Payment-related configuration when you connect providers (e.g. Paystack keys for your workspace). Secrets should be stored using strong server-side encryption in production environments.
privacy.p2: The web application uses secure, HTTP-only cookies for session continuity and CSRF-aware API access patterns where implemented. Avoid sharing browser profiles between individuals so sessions remain personal.
privacy.p3: Deployments may integrate third-party infrastructure such as email (SMTP), object storage for uploads, and payment processors. Your administrator chooses those providers and their regions; review their DPAs alongside this policy.
privacy.p4: Operational and audit data may be retained for as long as your workspace remains active and as required for compliance. Soft-deleted records may remain in databases for referential integrity until purged by your processes.
privacy.li4: Workspace owners can invite, suspend, or remove users.
privacy.li5: Users can update profile preferences where the product exposes them.
privacy.li6: Contact your workspace administrator for export or deletion requests.
privacy.p5: For privacy questions about a specific deployment, contact the organization that operates your {platform} instance. For product feedback, use the channels your team provides.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Rules for using {platform}. Replace placeholders with your legal entity and governing law before production marketing.
terms.p1: By accessing or using {platform} (“Service”), you agree to these Terms on behalf of yourself and, if applicable, the organization you represent. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
terms.p2: You must provide accurate information and keep credentials confidential. You are responsible for activity under your account. Workspace owners may invite or revoke access to their company data subject to product capabilities.
terms.li1: Do not attempt to access workspaces or data that are not yours.
terms.li2: Do not probe, scan, or disrupt the Service or other tenants.
terms.li3: Do not use the Service for unlawful or fraudulent purposes.
terms.li4: Comply with payment network and third-party provider rules you enable.
terms.p3: You retain rights to your business data. You grant the Service permission to host and process that data to provide features you use. You are responsible for compliance with laws applicable to your customers and employees.
terms.p4: We aim for reliable operation but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. Features may evolve; we may modify or discontinue parts of the Service with reasonable notice where appropriate.
terms.p5: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED “AS IS” WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW.
terms.p6: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, {platformUpper} AND ITS SUPPLIERS WILL NOT BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, OR GOODWILL. AGGREGATE LIABILITY FOR DIRECT DAMAGES SHOULD BE CAPPED AS SET FORTH IN YOUR ORDER FORM IF ANY; OTHERWISE, TO THE FEES PAID IN THE TWELVE MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM OR ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS, WHICHEVER IS GREATER.
terms.p7: Insert governing law and venue. Until then, consult qualified counsel before publishing these terms publicly.
